lesson.menu_desc: Describing trends with present continuous -- 29 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 9 -- Trends -- AI in Valuation -- Present Continuous (is/are + -ing)
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Marcos Mansour | Aula 9 -- Trends -- AI in Valuation

listenings.text: I want to share a few trends we are seeing in the valuation industry right now. Technology is changing the way we work. More and more firms are adopting artificial intelligence to process data. These tools are automating the slow, repetitive tasks, so our teams are spending less time on spreadsheets and more time on judgment. At the same time, the market is becoming more data-driven. Clients are asking for faster reports, and software is helping us forecast values with new models. Of course, AI is not replacing the appraiser. It is supporting us. The human still makes the final call. So the picture is clear: the industry is moving fast, and the firms that are adapting now are gaining a real advantage.

Honestly, I am a little worried about all this change. Everyone is talking about artificial intelligence, and sometimes I wonder if the machines are taking over our jobs. But I am trying to stay positive. I am learning the new software, and I am starting to see how it helps. The tools are getting better every month, and our clients are noticing the difference. I think the key is to keep adapting. The world is not slowing down, so neither am I.
